exampleSentencesTranslated: You can feel at ease because everything has been resolved.
When the doctor said the results were good, mom could feel at ease.
I will be by your side, so you can feel at ease.

translation.en: I feel at ease when at home.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi ở nhà.

definitions.es: Estar tranquilo o sentirse seguro y relajado.
definitions.vi: Cảm thấy yên tâm hoặc an toàn và thoải mái.
definitions.en: To be at ease or to feel secure and relaxed.

example.en: When you have finished your work, you can rest easy.
example.vi: Khi bạn đã hoàn thành công việc, hãy an tâm nghỉ ngơi.
example.es: Cuando hayas terminado tu trabajo, puedes descansar tranquilo.

notes:
An tâm means to feel at ease, reassured, not worried anymore. It’s about your mind being calm because you believe things are okay.

It’s usually used as a verb phrase (to feel reassured) or adjective-like state after verbs like cảm thấy, thấy, làm cho.  

Often paired with cứ to tell someone to relax: cứ an tâm.  
Close synonym: yên tâm (very similar, slightly more common in speech).  
Opposite idea: lo lắng, bất an.
exampleSentences: Em có thể an tâm vì mọi việc đã được giải quyết xong.
Khi nghe bác sĩ nói kết quả tốt, mẹ cảm thấy an tâm hơn.
Anh sẽ ở bên cạnh em, nên em cứ an tâm nhé.

translation.en: I feel at ease when at home.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi ở nhà.

notes_md:
**An tâm** means *to feel at ease, reassured, not worried anymore*. It’s about your mind being calm because you believe things are okay.

It’s usually used as a verb phrase (to feel reassured) or adjective-like state after verbs like *cảm thấy*, *thấy*, *làm cho*.  

Often paired with **cứ** to tell someone to relax: *cứ an tâm*.  
Close synonym: **yên tâm** (very similar, slightly more common in speech).  
Opposite idea: **lo lắng**, **bất an**.
